_meta.task: P3 W5-T2 — cross-vendor + agent routing re-run (no regression vs P2 matrix)
_meta.description: Recorded 9-way routing matrix for the P3 release HEAD. P3 ships NO new vendor and NO new agent (platform-only: HA/scale-out + WebSocket fan-out + worker changes), so the routable specialist roster and every per-agent tool allow-list are UNCHANGED from the P2 W5-T2 matrix. This file is the recorded artifact the deterministic no-regression gate (test_p3_routing_no_regression.py) pins the live P3-HEAD registry against, and the artifact the W5-T3 phase-exit gate doc (PRODUCTION.md 2.6 / G-MNT) cites. It is generated from build_default_registry() and cross-checked in-test against the P2 module's own published constants, so it is proven to BE the P2 matrix — not a tautological re-derivation of whatever the live registry happens to be.
_meta.provenance: Introspected from app.agents.build_default_registry() on branch feat/p3-w5-evals; cross-verified against backend/tests/agents/eval/test_p2_cross_vendor_routing.py constants. RECONCILED at P4 W2-T4 (feat/p4-w2-app-dependency-topology): the Troubleshooting Agent gains ONE new READ_ONLY tool, get_application_impact (ADR-0052 §8 application-dependency impact read) — a conscious, reviewed capability addition, not a silent rebaseline. The roster (9 agents, no new agent/vendor), the Security-Agent allow-list, and the state-changing-reachable set ({ddi, security}) are BYTE-IDENTICAL to the P2 matrix; only the troubleshooting read-only surface grew by one. RECONCILED again at P4 W3-T1 (feat/p4-w3-compliance-reporting): the Documentation Agent gains THREE READ_ONLY report tools — list_report_runs, get_report_run, request_report_generation (ADR-0053 §1: the agent triggers and cites compliance/audit reports under the invoking user's per-kind RBAC floor; it never authors artifact content) — a conscious, reviewed capability addition, not a silent rebaseline.

layers.deterministic_ci: This artifact + test_p3_routing_no_regression.py (roster/allow-list/injection-boundary no-regression structure) + test_p2_cross_vendor_routing.py + test_p1_prompt_injection.py ED1-ED5 (behavioural, StaticPool, in CI).
layers.real_llm_manual: Routing QUALITY (a real local model routes each held-out intent to the correct specialist) lives in test_routing_eval.py — module-skipped without NETOPS_RUN_ROUTING_EVAL=1; deferred-accepted when no local model is available. A scripted/registry re-run cannot prove routing quality, only roster/wiring no-regression.

injection_boundary.naming_note: ED1-ED5 are enumerated as fixture `objective` values and realized as TestED* CLASSES in test_p1_prompt_injection.py. They are NOT literal fixture case-IDs; fixture case-IDs use the pi-<target_agent>-<carrier>-<attack>-NNN scheme (plus one regression_anchor). W5-T2 spec asked to confirm the ED1-ED5 case-IDs live in the fixture — they do not appear as case-IDs; this mapping is the authoritative reconciliation.

result.allow_lists_unchanged_vs_p2: except one P4 W2-T4 addition: troubleshooting.get_application_impact (read_only, ADR-0052 §8) and three P4 W3-T1 additions: documentation.list_report_runs / get_report_run / request_report_generation (all read_only, ADR-0053 §1 Documentation-Agent report read/trigger tools under invoking-user RBAC); all other agents byte-identical to P2
